content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer NNN. Consider the sequence S=(1,2,…,N)S = (1, 2, \ldots, N)S=(1,2,…,N). You should choose two elements of this sequence and swap them.

A swap is nice if there is an integer MMM (1≤M<N1 \le M \lt N1≤M<N) such that the sum of the first MMM elements of the resulting sequence is equal to the sum of its last N−MN-MN−M elements. Find the number of nice swaps.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of nice swaps.

solution.approach: For each test case, iterate over all possible pairs of elements in the sequence. For each pair, calculate the sum of the first and last MMM elements of the resulting sequence. If the sums are equal, then the swap is nice. Count the number of nice swaps.
